source_article.title: Exploration of Multiple Signaling Pathways Through Which Sodium Tanshinone IIA Sulfonate Attenuates Pathologic Remodeling Experimental Infarction.
source_article.abstract: The level of maladaptive myocardial remodeling consistently contributes to the poor prognosis of patients following a myocardial infarction (MI). In this study, we investigated whether and how sodium tanshinone IIA sulfonate (STS) would attenuate the post-infarct cardiac remodeling in mice model of MI developing after surgical ligation of the left coronary artery. All mice subjected to experimental MI or to the sham procedure were then treated for the following 4 weeks, either with STS or with a vehicle alone. Results of our studies indicated that STS treatment of MI mice prevented the left ventricular dilatation and improved their cardiac function. Results of further tests, aimed at mechanistic explanation of the beneficial effects of STS, indicated that treatment with this compound enhanced the autophagy and, at the same time, inhibited apoptosis of the cardiomyocytes. Meaningfully, we have also established that myocardium of STS-treated mice displayed significantly higher levels of adenosine monophosphate kinase than their untreated counterparts and that this effect additionally associated with the significantly diminished activities of apoptotic promoters: mammalian target of rapamycin and P70S6 kinase. Moreover, we also found that additional administration of the adenosine monophosphate kinase inhibitor (compound C) or autophagy inhibitor (chloroquine) practically eliminated the observed beneficial effects of STS. In conclusion, we suggest that the described multistage mechanism triggered by STS treatment enhanced autophagy, thereby attenuating pathologic remodeling of the post-infarct hearts.

statements.text: In mice with myocardial infarction after left coronary artery ligation, sodium tanshinone IIA sulfonate attenuates post-infarct cardiac remodeling by preventing left ventricular dilatation and improving cardiac function compared with vehicle treatment.
statements.support_summary: Direct support comes from a mouse LAD-ligation MI study showing that sodium tanshinone IIA sulfonate (STS) improved echocardiographic cardiac function, reduced scar size/fibrosis markers, dampened early necrosis and inflammatory cytokines, and increased vascular density versus saline controls, consistent with attenuation of post-MI remodeling (PMID: 34951571). Another highly relevant item appears to be the original mouse MI study titled specifically on STS protection after MI (PMID: 30787992), but the abstract provided is truncated, so only limited support can be taken from it. Several related but less direct studies report similar benefits with tanshinone IIA formulations or in other species/models: nanoparticle tanshinone IIA prevented LV dilatation and improved function after coronary ligation in mice (PMID: 30284484), and rat or clinical studies reported reduced LV remodeling and/or improved cardiac function with STS/tanshinone IIA after MI or I/R injury (PMID: 29524303, PMID: 31066232, PMID: 24077633, PMID: 24911635, PMID: 28392904). Overall, the recurring themes are improved cardiac function, less fibrosis/infarct expansion, reduced inflammation/apoptosis, and in some studies explicit prevention of LV dilatation, although many supporting items are indirect because they use different formulations, species, or injury models.
statements.contradict_summary: The only contradicting item is a retraction notice for the source article itself (PMID: 40046745), which does not present new experimental data against the claim but indicates that the original supporting study should not be considered reliable evidence. This creates a major discrepancy: the claim matches the content of the retracted source study, yet the retraction undermines confidence in that specific report rather than showing opposite results. No abstract-based biological evidence demonstrating that STS worsens remodeling or fails to improve function in this mouse post-MI ligation model was provided.
statements.conclusion: The statement is overall more supported than contradicted based on the available abstracts, chiefly because one directly relevant mouse LAD-ligation study reports improved function and reduced remodeling with STS (PMID: 34951571), and multiple related studies in adjacent models show consistent cardioprotective effects. However, confidence is tempered because the source article underlying the claim has been retracted (PMID: 40046745), which weakens trust in that specific original evidence base.

statements.text: In mice with myocardial infarction after left coronary artery ligation, sodium tanshinone IIA sulfonate enhances cardiomyocyte autophagy and inhibits cardiomyocyte apoptosis compared with vehicle treatment.
statements.support_summary: Several preclinical studies report that sodium tanshinone IIA sulfonate (STS) reduces cardiomyocyte apoptosis and myocardial injury in rodent infarct/ischemia models, including decreased TUNEL-positive cells and caspase-3 activity with improved cardiac function and smaller infarcts (PMID: 24077633; PMID: 18427283; PMID: 30787992). One rat ischemia-reperfusion study also found increased autophagy markers (higher LC3B/LC3A ratio and Beclin-1) together with lower Bax/Caspase-3 and higher Bcl-2 after tanshinone IIA sodium sulfonate treatment, aligning most closely with the claim’s combined autophagy/apoptosis mechanism (PMID: 28392904). Additional indirect support comes from a related compound study showing tanshinone IIA acts through an AMPK/mTOR-dependent autophagy pathway after LAD ligation, but this is not STS specifically and therefore is only partially relevant (PMID: 30798134). A review also describes STS as having anti-apoptotic cardiovascular effects but does not directly test the claim (PMID: 31545252). Overall, the abstracts support anti-apoptotic effects strongly, whereas direct abstract-level evidence for enhanced autophagy in the exact mouse MI-after-ligation setting is limited and partly indirect.
statements.contradict_summary: The only contradicting item is a retraction notice for the source article itself, indicating that the original 2019 report should not be considered reliable evidence (PMID: 40046745). However, the retraction abstract provides no direct experimental findings that STS fails to enhance autophagy or inhibit apoptosis, so it is better interpreted as undermining confidence in that specific study rather than offering substantive biological contradiction. This creates an important discrepancy: the main direct mouse-MI evidence appears retracted, while several separate rodent studies still report similar anti-apoptotic and sometimes pro-autophagic effects.
statements.conclusion: The statement is overall more supported than contradicted, because multiple independent rodent studies describe STS-associated reductions in cardiomyocyte apoptosis and one closely related study also reports increased autophagy markers. However, confidence is tempered because the most directly matching source study has been retracted and much of the remaining evidence comes from rats, ischemia-reperfusion models, reviews, or related compounds rather than the exact mouse LAD-ligation model.

statements.text: In mice with myocardial infarction after left coronary artery ligation, the beneficial cardiac effects of sodium tanshinone IIA sulfonate depend on adenosine monophosphate kinase activity and autophagy, with inhibition of either pathway eliminating the protective effects.
statements.support_summary: One supporting study reports that in a mouse heart-failure model induced by left anterior descending coronary ligation, tanshinone IIA protected against post-myocardial infarction heart failure through an AMPK/mTOR-dependent autophagy pathway, supporting the general mechanism that AMPK activation and autophagy mediate cardioprotection (PMID: 30798134). However, from the provided abstract snippet, it is not clear whether this study specifically tested sodium tanshinone IIA sulfonate rather than tanshinone IIA itself, nor whether pharmacologic inhibition of AMPK or autophagy fully abolished the benefit, so support for those exact elements of the claim is only partial. Overall, the available supporting evidence is mechanistically aligned but not a direct replication of the precise statement.
statements.contradict_summary: There are no contradicting evidence items provided.
statements.conclusion: The claim is somewhat supported, but only weakly to moderately by the evidence provided. The single cited study is consistent with a cardioprotective role for tanshinone IIA mediated by AMPK-dependent autophagy (PMID: 30798134), yet it does not clearly establish from the abstract alone the specific sodium tanshinone IIA sulfonate formulation or that blocking either AMPK or autophagy eliminates protection.

matched_article.title: Shengui Sansheng San Ameliorates Cerebral Energy Deficiency via Citrate Cycle After Ischemic Stroke.
matched_article.abstract: Cerebral energy deficiency is a key pathophysiologic cascade that results in neuronal injury and necrosis after ischemic stroke. Shengui Sansheng San (SSS) has been used to treat stroke for more than 300 years. In present study, we investigated the therapeutic efficacy and mechanism of SSS extraction on cerebral energy deficiency post-stroke. In permanent middle cerebral artery occlusion (pMCAo) model of rats, it suggested that SSS extraction in dose-dependent manner improved neurological function, cerebral blood flow (CBF), 18F-2-deoxy-glucose uptake and the density and diameter of alpha smooth muscle actin (α-SMA) positive vasculature in ipsilateral area, as well as decreased infarcted volume. Meanwhile, the metabolomics study in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) was performed by using 5-(diisopropylamino)amylamine (DIAAA) derivatization-UHPLC-Q-TOF/MS approach. Eighty-eight endogenous metabolites were identified, and mainly involved in citrate cycle, fatty acid biosynthesis, aminoacyl-tRNA biosynthesis, amino acids metabolism and biosynthesis, etc. The remarkable increase of citrate in CSF after treatment with three dosages indicated that the therapeutic mechanism of SSS extraction might be related with citrate cycle. Simultaneously, it showed that high dosage group significantly increased peripheral blood glucose level, the expressions of glucose transporter (GLUT) 1, GLUT3, and monocarboxylic acid transporter 1 (MCT1), which contributed to the transportation of glucose and lactate. By the regulations of phosphorylated pyruvate dehydrogenase E1-alpha (p-PDHA1), acetyl CoA synthetase and citrate synthetase (CS), the levels of citrate and its upstream molecules (pyruvate and acetyl CoA) in peri-infarction zone further enhanced, which ultimately caused the massive yield of adenosine triphosphate (ATP). Our study first demonstrated that SSS extraction could ameliorate cerebral energy deficiency after ischemia by citrate cycle, which is characterized by the enhancements of glucose supply, transportation, utilization, and metabolism.

statements.text: In rats with permanent middle cerebral artery occlusion after ischemic stroke, Shengui Sansheng San extraction improved neurological function, cerebral blood flow, brain glucose uptake, and vascular markers in the ischemic hemisphere while reducing infarct volume compared with untreated ischemic rats.
statements.support_summary: Two rat permanent MCAO studies directly support key parts of the claim for Shengui Sansheng San/Pulvis extracts: SSS extraction improved neurological scores and reduced infarct volume, while also increasing angiogenesis-related vascular markers including vWF, SDF-1α/CXCR4, and VEGF/VEGFR2 in the ischemic penumbra (PMID: 29895489). A related permanent MCAO study of Shengui Sansheng Pulvis reported improved motor/balance outcomes, reduced brain edema, and preservation of endothelial/BBB-associated markers via VIP signaling, which is consistent with vascular protection but does not directly address infarct size, cerebral blood flow, or glucose uptake (PMID: 31999981). Another study using the closely related formulation Ginseng-Angelica-Sansheng-Pulvis found improved regional blood flow, reduced infarction volume, and better neurological outcomes after MCAO, but the formulation name differs from the exact claim and the abstract does not mention glucose uptake or the same vascular markers (PMID: 31191223). Across these studies, recurring themes are improved functional recovery, smaller infarcts, and enhanced vascular/endothelial repair after ischemic stroke, but direct abstract-level evidence for increased brain glucose uptake is limited or absent in the cited items.
statements.contradict_summary: No contradicting evidence items were provided.
statements.conclusion: The statement is overall more supported than contradicted because multiple rat MCAO studies on Shengui Sansheng-derived formulations report improved neurological outcomes, reduced infarct volume, and enhanced vascular/endothelial markers, with one related study also showing improved regional blood flow. However, support is incomplete for the full claim because the provided abstracts do not directly demonstrate brain glucose uptake changes, and one supportive study uses a closely related but not identically named formulation.

statements.text: In rats with permanent middle cerebral artery occlusion after ischemic stroke, Shengui Sansheng San extraction increased citrate-cycle-related metabolites and enzymes in the peri-infarction region, indicating enhanced cerebral energy metabolism compared with untreated ischemic rats.

source_article.title: Social success of perfumes.
source_article.abstract: We study data on perfumes and their odour descriptors-notes-to understand how note compositions, called accords, influence successful fragrance formulas. We obtain accords which tend to be present in perfumes that receive significantly more customer ratings. Our findings show that the most popular notes and the most over-represented accords are different to those that have the strongest effect to the perfume ratings. We also used network centrality to understand which notes have the highest potential to enhance note compositions. We find that large degree notes, such as musk and vanilla as well as generically-named notes, e.g. floral notes, are amongst the notes that enhance accords the most. This work presents a framework which would be a timely tool for perfumers to explore a multidimensional space of scent compositions.

statements.text: Perfume formulas with certain note combinations are associated with higher customer ratings, indicating that some accords contribute to greater fragrance success.
statements.support_summary: One small experimental study in cosmetics found that specific olfactory notes were needed to create fragrance-texture congruent pairings and that congruent products elicited the highest hedonic responses and improved overall product appreciation after short-term use (PMID: 37073417). This provides indirect support for the idea that certain combinations of fragrance notes can be linked to greater product success, but the study examined multimodal congruence and hedonic evaluation rather than perfume formulas, customer ratings, or market success directly. Thus, the available support is limited in scope and only partially aligned with the claim.
statements.contradict_summary: No contradicting evidence items were provided.
statements.conclusion: The statement is weakly supported overall. The only available study suggests that certain olfactory combinations can increase hedonic appreciation in a cosmetic context, but it does not directly test perfume note combinations against customer ratings or fragrance success.

statements.text: The notes and accords most frequently present in popular perfumes are not necessarily the same as the notes and accords with the strongest positive association with perfume ratings.

statements.contradict_summary: No non-zero contradicting evidence items were available.
statements.conclusion: This statement had only zero-score or missing-score rows in the input file.

matched_article.title: Ultrasonic vocalization of pup and adult fat-tailed gerbils (Pachyuromys duprasi).
matched_article.abstract: Ultrasonic vocalizations (USVs) of laboratory rodents indicate animal emotional arousal and may serve as models of human disorders. We analysed spectrographically USV calls of pup and adult fat-tailed gerbils Pachyuromys duprasi during 420-s tests, including isolation, touch and handling. Based on combination of six different USV syllable contour shapes and six different note compositions, we classified 782 USV syllables of 24 pups aged 5-10 days to 18 types and 232 syllables of 7 adults to 24 types. Pups and adults shared 16 of these 26 USV types. Percentages of USV syllables with certain contour shapes differed between pups and adults. The contour shape and note composition significantly affected most acoustic variables of USV syllables in either pups or adults. The 1-note USV syllables were most common in either pups or adults. Pup USV syllables were overall longer and higher-frequency than adult ones, reminiscent of the USV ontogenetic pathway of bats and distinctive to rats and mice. We discuss that the USV syllable types of fat-tailed gerbils were generally similar in contour shapes and note compositions with USV syllable types of mice and rats, what means that software developed for automated classifying of mice ultrasound might be easily adapted or re-tuned to gerbil USV calls. However, using fat-tailed gerbils as model for biomedical research including control of USV vocalization is only possible since 6th day of pup life, because of the delayed emergence of USV calls in ontogeny of this species.

statements.text: Pup and adult fat-tailed gerbils produce ultrasonic vocalization syllables that are broadly similar in contour shapes and note compositions to ultrasonic vocalization syllables of mice and rats, suggesting that software developed for automated classification of mouse ultrasound could be adapted for gerbil calls.

statements.text: Pup fat-tailed gerbil ultrasonic vocalization syllables are generally longer and higher in frequency than adult fat-tailed gerbil syllables, indicating an age-related developmental change in vocalization structure.
statements.support_summary: No supporting evidence items were provided.
statements.contradict_summary: One cited study does not clearly contradict the claim and may be miscategorized. The abstract for PMID: 32971224 focuses mainly on audible calls and explicitly states that previously documented ultrasonic calls of fat-tailed gerbils show an ontogenetic pathway toward higher-frequency and shorter calls, which is broadly consistent with developmental change rather than contradiction. Within the reported audible calls, pup high-frequency squeaks were longer, lower in fundamental frequency, and higher in peak frequency than adult calls, while clicks did not differ acoustically, indicating mixed age effects rather than a direct refutation of the ultrasonic claim (PMID: 32971224). Overall, this item provides only indirect and partly discrepant evidence relative to the specific statement about pup versus adult ultrasonic syllables.
statements.conclusion: The evidence is unclear but leans away from contradiction because the only listed contradicting study appears miscategorized and does not directly refute the claim. With no direct supporting items provided and only one indirect, mixed abstract, the statement cannot be strongly evaluated from this evidence set.

source_article.title: Effects of Tai Chi on Patients with Mild Cognitive Impairment: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
source_article.abstract: DESIGN: Randomized controlled trials (RCTs) from multiple databases from inception to December 2020 published in English were searched. Two researchers independently performed eligible study screening and data extraction. The methodological quality was assessed with the Jadad score. Meta-analysis of RCTs on TC in the treatment of MCI was performed with RevMan Version 5.4.1. RESULTS: Seven RCTs with 1265 participants were included. For most RCTs, the overall reporting of methodological quality was high. Results of the meta-analysis indicate that TC improved MCI patients' cognitive function significantly, including overall cognitive function (MD = -2.24, 95% CI -3.51 to -0.97, P = 0.0005), memory and learning (SMD = 0.83, 95% CI 0.22 to 1.45, P = 0.008), visuospatial ability (MD = 3.15, 95% CI 0.74 to 5.56, P = 0.01), executive functions (MD = 0.32, 95% CI 0.03 to 0.61, P = 0.03), and physical activity (MD = 18.78, 95% CI 10.80 to 26.76, P < 0.00001). However, no significant benefit was found for TC on psychological activity (MD = 0.17, 95% CI -0.62 to 0.96, P = 0.36) and biomarker improvement. CONCLUSION: The meta-analysis confirmed the clinical therapeutic effect of TC for MCI. More rigorous and long-term follow-up RCTs should be conducted in the future.

statements.text: Tai chi improves overall cognitive function, memory and learning, visuospatial ability, and executive function in patients with mild cognitive impairment compared with no tai chi or usual care.
statements.support_summary: Multiple systematic reviews/meta-analyses focused on MCI report that Tai Chi improves global cognition and several specific domains, including memory/learning, visuospatial function, and executive function, although many note moderate study quality, small samples, and risk of bias (PMID: 37728939, PMID: 32308706, PMID: 33043709). Direct randomized trials in MCI or amnestic MCI also found benefits versus control/usual care-like comparators for global cognition and some domains, especially memory and executive function, with one recent multicenter RCT also linking memory gains to hippocampal/plasticity markers (PMID: 29353543, PMID: 38824049, PMID: 37022680). Broader reviews of Tai Chi/Qigong or mind-body exercise in neurological disorders and older adults are generally directionally supportive but are less specific to Tai Chi alone or to MCI specifically, so they provide more indirect support (PMID: 35704955, PMID: 30986409, PMID: 30565212, PMID: 37280512). Some supporting items are only tangential because they evaluate mind-body exercise, traditional Chinese exercise, or exercise overall rather than Tai Chi specifically, creating a specificity discrepancy with the claim (PMID: 37399446, PMID: 32756073, PMID: 32460123, PMID: 35653946). Overall, the strongest support comes from MCI-specific Tai Chi meta-analyses and RCTs, especially for global cognition, memory, visuospatial ability, and executive function.
statements.contradict_summary: Contradictory evidence mainly emphasizes uncertainty rather than clear harm: a recent scoping review concluded that findings for global cognition, executive function, memory/language, and perceptual-motor outcomes in MCI/early dementia remain inconsistent and overall inconclusive (PMID: 37891692). An earlier meta-analysis of traditional Chinese exercise in MCI found no significant effects on global cognition, memory, or executive function, though it did find improved visuospatial function, making it only a partial contradiction to the full claim (PMID: 30762810). Another review of combined Tai Chi plus cognitive intervention reported gains in memory and overall cognition but no significant benefit in executive function, and it is not directly testing Tai Chi alone versus usual care, so its placement as contradiction is somewhat discrepant (PMID: 35439549). A general exercise meta-analysis in MCI found little evidence for memory or executive benefits overall, but because it was not Tai Chi-specific it is only indirect contradiction to this claim (PMID: 23831175).
statements.conclusion: The statement is overall more supported than contradicted. MCI-specific Tai Chi systematic reviews/meta-analyses and several randomized trials consistently indicate improvements in global cognition, memory, visuospatial ability, and executive/executive-related function, while the contradictory literature mainly reflects older or broader reviews concluding inconsistency or insufficient certainty rather than strong evidence of no effect.

statements.text: Tai chi improves physical activity in patients with mild cognitive impairment compared with no tai chi or usual care.
statements.support_summary: One randomized/controlled study in older adults with cognitive impairment found that virtual reality-based Tai Chi improved multiple physical performance measures versus control over 6 months, including 6-minute walk, sit-to-stand, functional reach, and gait speed, though the population was broader cognitive impairment rather than explicitly mild cognitive impairment and outcomes were physical function rather than overall physical activity (PMID: 30537752). A systematic review of eight RCTs in cognitively impaired older adults reported that combined physical-cognitive interventions, including mind-body Tai Chi, improved balance and gait speed versus control, with significantly better effects on balance and gait speed within mild cognitive impairment populations, although the abstract does not isolate Tai Chi-specific effects from other multicomponent interventions (PMID: 27532469). A broader meta-analysis of 17 randomized trials found Tai Chi/Qigong improved physical function in older adults overall, but it was not specific to mild cognitive impairment and pooled Tai Chi with Qigong, so it is indirect support only (PMID: 37280512). Overall, the supportive evidence trends toward better physical functioning with Tai Chi-related interventions, but there is a mismatch between the claim's specificity (mild cognitive impairment, physical activity, versus usual care/no Tai Chi) and what several abstracts directly report.
statements.contradict_summary: There are no contradicting evidence items provided.
statements.conclusion: The statement is overall more supported than contradicted, because the available evidence consistently points toward improved physical performance/function with Tai Chi-related interventions and includes some relevance to cognitively impaired and mild cognitive impairment populations. However, support is only moderate because much of the evidence is indirect, with limited abstracts directly testing standard Tai Chi versus usual care specifically for mild cognitive impairment and physical activity outcomes.

statements.text: Tai chi does not provide clear benefit for psychological outcomes or biomarker improvement in patients with mild cognitive impairment compared with no tai chi or usual care.
statements.support_summary: A scoping review focused on MCI/early dementia found inconsistent neurocognitive results and reported no significant between-group difference in depressive symptoms, concluding that Tai Chi effects remain inconclusive (PMID: 37891692). A broader meta-analysis in older adults, not specific to MCI, found no significant benefit on the psychological component of quality of life despite improvements in overall and physical quality of life; this is only indirectly relevant to the claim and does not address biomarkers (PMID: 32901684). Overall, the supporting evidence mainly suggests lack of clear psychological benefit, but it is limited by indirect populations/outcomes and by not directly addressing biomarker improvement.
statements.contradict_summary: Most contradicting studies instead report benefits of Tai Chi for cognitive outcomes in MCI, including multiple systematic reviews/meta-analyses showing improvements in global cognition, memory, executive function, attention, and visuospatial ability versus nonintervention, usual care, or active controls (PMID: 33043709, PMID: 32308706, PMID: 37728939, PMID: 35653946, PMID: 35704955, PMID: 24383523). Several randomized trials in MCI/aMCI also found cognitive benefits, such as improved memory or global cognition, compared with control or education; one trial additionally found increased plasma BDNF (PMID: 29353543), and another reported increased hippocampal volume, altered hippocampal connectivity, and exosomal LRP1 changes associated with memory improvement (PMID: 38824049). A large RCT in older adults with type 2 diabetes plus MCI found Tai Chi improved MoCA scores versus fitness walking at 36 weeks, though this compares Tai Chi with another exercise rather than usual care (PMID: 37022680). Some evidence is less directly applicable because it combines Tai Chi with other mind-body exercises or includes broader cognitive impairment populations rather than strictly MCI (PMID: 30565212, PMID: 37653776, PMID: 30986409, PMID: 30537752), but the dominant pattern is benefit rather than no clear effect. Notably, several contradicting papers concern cognition more than psychological symptoms specifically, so there is some outcome mismatch with the claim.
statements.conclusion: The statement is overall more contradicted than supported. Although one MCI-focused review found psychological effects inconclusive and no clear antidepressant benefit, the larger body of evidence—especially multiple MCI-focused reviews and randomized trials—shows Tai Chi improves cognitive outcomes and, in some studies, relevant biomarkers/neurobiological measures, which conflicts with the claim that no clear benefit exists.

matched_article.title: Genes That Predict Poor Prognosis in Breast Cancer via Bioinformatical Analysis.
matched_article.abstract: Breast cancer is one of the most commonly diagnosed cancers all over the world, and it is now the leading cause of cancer death among females. The aim of this study was to find DEGs (differentially expressed genes) which can predict poor prognosis in breast cancer and be effective targets for breast cancer patients via bioinformatical analysis. GSE86374, GSE5364, and GSE70947 were chosen from the GEO database. DEGs between breast cancer tissues and normal breast tissues were picked out by GEO2R and Venn diagram software. Then, DAVID (Database for Annotation, Visualization, and Integrated Discovery) was used to analyze these DEGs in gene ontology (GO) including molecular function (MF), cellular component (CC), and biological process (BP) and Kyoto Encyclopedia of Gene and Genome (KEGG) pathway. Next, STRING (Search Tool for the Retrieval of Interacting Genes) was used to investigate potential protein-protein interaction (PPI) relationships among DEGs and these DEGs were analyzed by Molecular Complex Detection (MCODE) in Cytoscape. After that, UALCAN, GEPIA (gene expression profiling interactive analysis), and KM (Kaplan-Meier plotter) were used for the prognostic information and core genes were qualified. There were 96 upregulated genes and 98 downregulated genes in this study. 55 upregulated genes were selected as hub genes in the PPI network. For validation in UALCAN, GEPIA, and KM, 5 core genes (KIF4A, RACGAP1, CKS2, SHCBP1, and HMMR) were found to highly expressed in breast cancer tissues with poor prognosis. They differentially expressed between different subclasses of breast cancer. These five genes (KIF4A, RACGAP1, CKS2, SHCBP1, and HMMR) could be potential targets for therapy in breast cancer and prediction of prognosis on the basis of bioinformatical analysis.

statements.text: Breast cancer tissues, compared with normal breast tissues, show higher expression of KIF4A, RACGAP1, CKS2, SHCBP1, and HMMR, and elevated expression of these genes is associated with poorer prognosis.
statements.support_summary: One bioinformatic study found KIF4A to be upregulated in breast cancer datasets and associated with worse overall survival, with additional correlations to higher stage and tumor category, supporting the KIF4A portion of the claim (PMID: 33291061). However, this abstract does not address RACGAP1, CKS2, SHCBP1, or HMMR, so support is limited to only one of the five genes.
statements.contradict_summary: One subtype-focused bioinformatic study reported that in basal-like breast cancer, lower CKS2 expression was associated with poor prognosis, which runs opposite to the claim that elevated expression predicts worse outcome (PMID: 33066779). That same abstract analyzes tumor versus normal tissue differentially expressed genes by subtype, but the provided abstract does not explicitly state whether CKS2 is upregulated relative to normal tissue, and it does not address KIF4A, RACGAP1, SHCBP1, or HMMR. Thus, the contradiction is partial and limited to the prognostic direction for CKS2 in one subtype.
statements.conclusion: The evidence is mixed but leans unclear overall. There is modest support for KIF4A being overexpressed and linked to poor prognosis, but the claim covers five genes and the only evidence touching CKS2 suggests the opposite prognostic association in a specific breast cancer subtype.

statements.text: Breast cancer subtypes differ in the expression of KIF4A, RACGAP1, CKS2, SHCBP1, and HMMR, indicating that these genes distinguish breast cancer subclasses.
statements.support_summary: No non-zero supporting evidence items were available.

statements.text: KIF4A, RACGAP1, CKS2, SHCBP1, and HMMR are potential prognostic biomarkers and therapeutic targets for breast cancer.
statements.support_summary: All four supporting papers are bioinformatics-driven breast cancer studies that identify subsets of the claimed genes as candidate prognostic markers, but none of the abstracts supports the full five-gene set together. KIF4A was identified as an upregulated hub gene associated with poor outcome and as an independent prognostic factor in breast cancer in one integrated WGCNA/PPI analysis (PMID: 33291061). RACGAP1 was highlighted as a hub gene linked to TNM stage-related modules and recurrence-free survival or prognosis in two systems-biology/bioinformatics studies of breast cancer, particularly HER2-negative disease in one paper (PMID: 32616754; PMID: 35116555). HMMR was identified as an overexpressed hub gene associated with poor survival and proposed as a potential therapeutic target in another breast cancer bioinformatics analysis (PMID: 36930083). However, no provided abstract mentions CKS2 or SHCBP1, so support for the complete claim is only partial and this is an important scope discrepancy.
statements.contradict_summary: No contradicting evidence items were provided.
statements.conclusion: The statement is overall more supported than contradicted, because several abstracts independently implicate KIF4A, RACGAP1, and HMMR as prognostic or targetable breast cancer-related hub genes. However, the evidence is incomplete for the exact claim since the provided studies do not address CKS2 or SHCBP1 in their abstracts, so support is moderate rather than strong.

source_article.title: Inhibition of microRNA-19b promotes ovarian granulosa cell proliferation by targeting IGF-1 in polycystic ovary syndrome.
source_article.abstract: The purpose of the present study was to investigate the functional role of microRNA (miR)-19b in polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) and try to elucidate its underlying mechanisms. Expression of miR‑19b and insulin‑like growth factor 1 (IGF-1) was examined in ovarian cortexes [(from 18 women with PCOS and 10 who did not have PCOS (non‑PCOS)] and KGN cells. Cell proliferation assays (cell viability and colony formation assay) were performed following overexpression or inhibition of miR‑19b and IGF‑1 or following insulin treatment in KGN cells. Expression levels of the cell cycle-associated protein cyclin D1 and cyclin‑dependent kinase (CDK) 1 were analyzed following overexpression or inhibition of miR-19b and IGF-1. Potential miR‑19b targets were identified by bioinformatics. Luciferase assay, reverse transcription‑quantitative polymerase chain reaction and western blotting were performed to determine whether IGF‑1 was a target of miR‑19b. miR‑19b expression was significantly decreased in the PCOS ovarian cortex and KGN cells and its identified target, IGF‑1, was upregulated. miR‑19b overexpression inhibited cell proliferation at G2/M phrase. Overexpression of IGF‑1 promoted cell viability and colony formation ability in KGN cells. The expression of cyclin D1 and CDK1 was statistically increased by inhibition of miR‑19b and overexpression of IGF‑1. High concentrations of insulin decreased levels of miR‑19b, stimulated KGN cell proliferation, and elevated IGF‑1 levels. Inhibition of miR‑19b promoted ovarian granulosa cell proliferation by targeting IGF‑1 in PCOS. Insulin decreased the expression levels of miR‑19b and stimulated cell proliferation. The present study suggested that overexpression of miR‑19b may be a potential therapeutic approach for PCOS.

statements.text: In polycystic ovary syndrome ovarian granulosa cells, decreased microRNA-19b promotes granulosa cell proliferation by derepressing insulin-like growth factor 1.

statements.text: In polycystic ovary syndrome ovarian granulosa cells, increased insulin signaling decreases microRNA-19b expression, increases insulin-like growth factor 1 expression, and stimulates granulosa cell proliferation.
statements.support_summary: No supporting evidence items were provided.
statements.contradict_summary: Several abstracts suggest insulin-related signaling in PCOS granulosa cells is associated with reduced proliferation or increased apoptosis rather than proliferation. Insulin upregulated lncRNA BANCR, and BANCR overexpression inhibited proliferation and promoted apoptosis in KGN/granulosa cells (PMID: 30592281). Insulin also induced PTEN and attenuated IGF-I-induced Akt phosphorylation and cell proliferation in human granulosa cells, with PTEN levels correlating with follicular-fluid insulin in PCOS patients (PMID: 19318457). Additional studies link altered insulin/PI3K-Akt pathway regulation to granulosa-cell apoptosis in PCOS, including LNK inhibition of insulin-stimulated AKT-FOXO3 signaling (PMID: 33495419), although this does not directly test the specific miR-19b/IGF-1 claim. One item appears miscategorized as contradiction because it actually reports that insulin dose-dependently suppresses miR-99a, while miR-99a overexpression reduces proliferation and IGF-1 can reverse that effect, which is broadly directionally compatible with insulin-linked proliferation but concerns miR-99a/IGF-1R rather than miR-19b/IGF-1 (PMID: 30374732); the ARID1A study is also only indirect and not specific to insulin or miR-19b (PMID: 33290750).
statements.conclusion: Overall, the claim is more contradicted than supported based on the provided evidence. The strongest relevant abstracts indicate insulin-associated pathways can inhibit granulosa-cell proliferation or promote apoptosis in PCOS-related contexts, while none directly support the specific sequence of decreased miR-19b, increased IGF-1, and stimulated proliferation.

statements.text: In polycystic ovary syndrome ovarian granulosa cells, increasing microRNA-19b expression inhibits cell proliferation, indicating that microRNA-19b may be a potential therapeutic target for polycystic ovary syndrome.
statements.support_summary: No supporting evidence items were provided.
statements.contradict_summary: One study in PCOS-related KGN granulosa-like cells reports that H19 overexpression decreases miR-19b and promotes proliferation while reducing apoptosis, whereas H19 silencing increases miR-19b and has the opposite effects, consistent with miR-19b acting to suppress proliferation rather than enhance it (PMID: 33053114). However, this abstract does not directly state that experimental miR-19b overexpression alone inhibits proliferation, so it is at best indirect evidence rather than a clean contradiction. This creates a discrepancy with the item's contradiction label and explanation, because the abstract appears more compatible with the claim than opposed to it (PMID: 33053114).
statements.conclusion: The evidence is unclear but leans away from contradiction because the only cited abstract is indirectly consistent with miR-19b suppressing granulosa-cell proliferation, despite being categorized as contradicting. With no direct supporting studies provided and only one misaligned indirect study, the claim remains insufficiently established here.

matched_article.title: High LIN28A and PLK4 co‑expression is associated with poor prognosis in epithelial ovarian cancer.
matched_article.abstract: Epithelial ovarian cancer (EOC) is the most lethal gynecological malignancy. LIN28 homolog A (LIN28A) is a RNA‑binding protein, which serves a fundamental role in cell development and pluripotency. Polo‑like kinase 4 (PLK4) is a member of the polo‑like kinase family, which primarily takes part in the mitotic regulation. Overexpression of LIN28A has been demonstrated in ovarian cancer; however, the expression of PLK4 and the correlation between the expression of LIN28A and PLK4 in EOC has not been discussed. In the present study, the mRNA and protein levels of LIN28A and PLK4 were evaluated by reverse transcription‑quantitative polymerase chain reaction and immunohistochemistry in ovarian tissues of patients. Results demonstrated significantly increased expression in EOC compared with benign epithelial ovarian tumors. High expression of LIN28A and PLK4 was detected at the advanced pathological stage. Furthermore, PLK4 expression was positively correlated with LIN28A (r=0.555; P=0.039). The median survival analysis of patients with EOC with LIN28A and PLK4 double positive expression was 14 months, compared with 30 months in single positive and 60 months in double negative patients by Kaplan‑Meier analysis (P<0.05). The expressions of LIN28A and PLK4 was elevated in different EOC cell lines compared to with a normal ovarian cell line. The 293T cells transfected with LIN28A plus a PLK4 plasmid were the fastest‑growing group. These results suggest that co‑expression of LIN28A and PLK4 may be associated with poor prognosis of EOC and could serve as promising prognostic biomarkers and therapeutic targets in EOC. LIN28A and PLK4 may be used along with traditional morphological and clinical characteristics for predicting prognosis.

statements.text: In epithelial ovarian cancer, co-expression of LIN28A and PLK4 is associated with poorer prognosis than single-positive or double-negative expression patterns.
